question:
Hull City AFC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for November 25 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/
event_title:
Hull City AFC vs. Ipswich Town FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC, scheduled for November 25 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Hull City AFC and Ipswich Town FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/

They've shown an ability to score goals, as seen in their victories over Portsmouth and Norwich, but their defence has been leaky, conceding three goals against QPR. This suggests they might adopt an attacking approach against Ipswich, hoping to outscore their opponents. However, they'll need to tighten up at the back to avoid another defeat. evidences.title: Preview: Hull City vs Ipswich Town - prediction, team news, lineups
evidences.content: Hull were set to be without Kasey Palmer, Eliot Matazo and Liam Millar, while Oli McBurnie was not expected to return in time. John Lundstram was hoping to prove his fitness, and Matt Crooks was suspended after his fifth yellow card of the season. Ipswich were missing Alex Palmer, Harry Clarke, Conor Townsend and Wes Burns through injury, while Sammie Szmodics was not expected to be ready. Marcelino Nunez and George Hirst were options if changes were made.
